examples.title: View Transformation Matrix Generation
examples.description: Algorithm to compute the view matrix from camera parameters
examples.explanation: The view matrix transforms world coordinates to camera space. It is constructed using the camera position (eye), look-at point, and up vector. The matrix aligns the world with the camera's coordinate system.

examples.title: Perspective Projection Matrix Generation
examples.description: Algorithm to create perspective projection matrix
examples.code:
FUNCTION ComputePerspectiveMatrix(fov, aspect, near, far)
 // Convert fov from degrees to radians
 fovRad = fov * (PI / 180)
 
 // Compute scale factors
 f = 1.0 / tan(fovRad / 2.0)
 
 // Build 4x4 perspective projection matrix
 projMatrix = [
 f / aspect, 0, 0, 0,
 0, f, 0, 0,
 0, 0, (far + near) / (near - far), -1,
 0, 0, (2 * far * near) / (near - far), 0
 ]
 
 RETURN projMatrix
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: The perspective projection matrix transforms camera coordinates to clip coordinates. It creates the foreshortening effect where objects appear smaller with distance. The matrix sets up the w component for perspective divide.

examples.title: Frustum Culling Check
examples.description: Algorithm to check if a bounding box is inside the viewing frustum
examples.code:
FUNCTION IsInFrustum(box, frustum)
 // Test all 8 corners of bounding box against each plane
 FOR each plane in frustum.planes
 outsideCount = 0
 FOR each corner in box.corners
 // Compute distance from plane
 distance = plane.normal.x * corner.x +
 plane.normal.y * corner.y +
 plane.normal.z * corner.z +
 plane.distance
 
 IF distance < 0 THEN
 outsideCount = outsideCount + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 // If all corners are outside one plane, box is outside frustum
 IF outsideCount == 8 THEN
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN TRUE
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: Frustum culling is an optimization technique that determines if objects are within the viewing volume before rendering. This saves GPU processing by skipping objects that wouldn't be visible anyway.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n = number of planes × 8 corners
